rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LIGHTFOOT VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A X-Complt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 5, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANT UNION PACIFIC RAILROAD COMPANY and NATIONAL RAILROAD PASSENGER CORPORATION'S (AMTRAK) MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A CROSS COMPLAINT. The motion to file a cross complaint is denied. Defendants delayed bringing this motion and allowing the filing at this stage of the proceedings would prejudice Catellus, McGuire & Hester, and St. Francis Electric, Inc., who have little time to conduct discovery on these claims and/or file a motion for summary judgment or adjudication. The denial of this motion will not prejudice Defendants because they can file an independent action seeking indemnity. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SALVATORE MINEO INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF ALL et al VS. CITY SIGHTSEEING CORPORATION, A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Class Certification; Memo Of P&A;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 5, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFFS SALVATORE MINEO INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED, GINA SCHEMBARI INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHER SIMILARLY SITUATED, PHILLIP NAILS, BENJAMIN DAUX and DAVID HAYWARD'S Motion For Class Certification. Granted in part. At the August 30 hearing certification was granted as to classes one and two (front desk/inside sales employees and tour guides) as to unpaid overtime under California law. This ruling was intended to cover both claims under both the Labor Code and the UCL, and is now re-affirmed. Plaintiffs seek certification for classes one and two for "misclassification as independent contractors." Other than possibly issues relating to the proximity of the trial date, it does not appear that defendant objects to such requests and thus these requests are granted without prejudice to defendant moving to continue the trial by way of motion or application made in department 206. The principal issue for this motion appears to be the issue as to which the court permitted supplemental briefing and continued the hearing on the initial motion for class certification, i.e. whether proposed class three (bus drivers) should be certified as to unpaid overtime under the UCL. Defendant's arguments against such certification go to the merits of the case and may be properly adjudicated by way of a motion for summary judgment/adjudication, but not a motion for class certification. Since the proposed class three meets all the requisites for class certification for asserted violations of the UCL as to both unpaid overtime and misclassification of independent contractors, class three is certified for these bases. All other relief sought in the initial motion for class certification is denied. Plaintiffs' counsel is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MANSA CONSTRUCTION CORPORATION DBA GREEN BANKER VS. WILFRED SHAW
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 5, 2013, Line 20, RESPONDENT WILFRED SHAW MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Motion to vacate judgment denied. Petitioner Mansa Construction Corporation filed a petition to confirm arbitration award. Petitioner also filed and served a civil case cover sheet, petition to confirm arbitration award, final award, notice of hearing on petition to confirm arbitration award, memorandum of points and authorities, and declaration of J. Scott McKay on Respondent Shaw. All of these documents together will be referred to collectively as the "Petition and supporting papers." CCP §1290.4(b) required that the Petition and supporting papers be served "in the manner provided by law for the service of summons in an action." (CCP §1290.4(b)(1)). The Petition and supporting papers were served by substitute service on Shaw on September 4, 2009. CCP § 415.20(a). This included mailing a copy of the Petition and supporting papers. Id. "Service of a summons in this manner is deemed complete on the 10th day after the mailing." CCP 415.20(a), which was September 14, 2009. From September 14, 2009, Shaw was then entitled to 10 days to respond to the Petition. CCP §1290.2. That section provides that "[a] petition under this title shall be heard in a summary way in the manner and upon the notice provided by law for the making and hearing of motions, except that not less than 10 days' notice of the date set for the hearing on the petition shall be given." CCP 1290.2. Shaw contends correctly that the hearing on the Petition should not have been noticed at least until September 24, 2009, when he had at least 10 days notice, counting from the date service of the Petition and supporting papers was effective. (Shaw also contends that another 5 days for service by mail under CCP 1013(b) should have been added, such that the hearing could not be held until September 29, 2009. As set forth below, I do not need to reach that issue.) The hearing was noticed for and the Petition to confirm arbitration award was heard on September 21, 2009. The arbitration award was confirmed by Order of the Court on September 21, 2009. Judgment confirming the arbitration award was dated that day and filed on September 22, 2009. (See part 2 of ruling.) = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: YIBRAH TESFA VS. ANNE STAUBOLL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner/Plaintiff Yibrah Tesfa'S Order To Show Cause Why Peremptory Writ Should Not Issue (Per Ex Parte Order Of 12/21/12)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 5, 2013, Line 10, Petitioner/Plaintiff Yibrah Tesfa'S Order To Show Cause Why Peremptory Writ Should Not Issue (Per Ex Parte Order Of 12/21/12). Continued on the court's own motion to March 5, 2013 in order to permit CalPERS the opportunity to file its opposition and provide courtesy copies per Local Rule 2.6B. On December 21, 2012, the court issued an alternative writ. The parties were ordered to file and serve any opposition and reply in accordance with the Code of Civil Procedure and to provide courtesy copies Department 302 on the day of filing. The Register of Actions does not reflect that CalPERS filed any opposition, and Department 302 did not receive any courtesy copies. However, on January 31, 2013, Petitioner filed a reply that refers to arguments raised in the opposition. Thus, CalPERS shall file its opposition papers that were previously served on Petitioner no later than February 6, 2013 at 4:00 pm. CalPERS shall provide courtesy copies of all opposing papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, on the same day as filing. The court has received courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 5, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 3, DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Respond To Requests For Admissions, Set One And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFFS, JESSE MORALES AND KATHERINE MORALES, HAVE FILED NO OPPOSITION TO THIS MOTION; ACCORDINGLY, DEFENDANTS¿ MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS TO RESPOND TO DEFENDANT¿S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS, SET ONE, IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS WILL FILE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES NO LATER THAN 15 DAYS AFTER THE DATE OF THIS ORDER; FAILURE TO DO SO WILL RESULT IN THE REQUESTS WILL BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND PLAINTIFFS WILL BE PRECLUDED FROM INTRODUCING ANY EVIDENCE CONTRARY TO THE FACTS ASSERT THEREIN. MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED ON PLAINTIFFS IN THE AMOUNT OF $252.00 WHICH WILL BE PAID TO DEFENDANTS WITHIN 10 DAYS OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR THE MOVING PARTY WILL PREPARE AND SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS AN APPROPRIATE FORM OF ORDER FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND SUBMIT IT TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON FEBRUARY 5, 2013. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Pltfs To Respond To Requests For Admissions, Set Two And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 5, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 3, DEFENDANT REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Respond To Requests For Admissions, Set Two And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFFS, JESSE MORALES AND KATHERINE MORALES, HAVE FILED NO OPPOSITION TO THIS MOTION; ACCORDINGLY, DEFENDANTS¿ MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS TO RESPOND TO DEFENDANT¿S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS, SET TWO, IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS WILL FILE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES NO LATER THAN 15 DAYS AFTER THE DATE OF THIS ORDER; FAILURE TO DO SO WILL RESULT IN THE REQUESTS WILL BE DEEMED ADMITTED AND PLAINTIFFS WILL BE PRECLUDED FROM INTRODUCING ANY EVIDENCE CONTRARY TO THE FACTS ASSERT THEREIN. MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED ON PLAINTIFFS IN THE AMOUNT OF $252.00 WHICH WILL BE PAID TO DEFENDANTS WITHIN 10 DAYS OF THIS ORDER. COUNSEL FOR THE MOVING PARTY WILL PREPARE AND SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS AN APPROPRIATE FORM OF ORDER FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND SUBMIT IT TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON FEBRUARY 5, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 5, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 5, DEFENDANT SATINDER SINGH'S Motion For Leave To Re-Open Discovery Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. rulings.Case Title: STEVEN A. BURMEISTER, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE VS. 4055 SONOMA PROPERTIES, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Steven Burmeister'S Motion To Tax The Second Amended Memo Of Costs Submitted By Diana Saca And 4055 Sonoma Properties, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 5, 2013, Line 20, Plaintiff Steven Burmeister's Motion To Tax The Second Amended Memo Of Costs Submitted By Diana Saca And 4055 Sonoma Properties, LLC. Off calendar as to the portion of the motion seeking to tax claimed attorney's fees, granted as to the portions of the motion seeking to tax costs claimed on line 11 and the amounts included in line 13 relating to document discovery and Federal Express charges, and denied as to the portions of the motion seeking to tax costs claimed on line 5 and the amount included in line 13 relating to mediation fees. Defendants' entitlement to attorney's fees will be adjudicated on defendants' motion for fees. Defendants' claimed costs for mediation fees were reasonably incurred and are allowed in the exercise of the court's discretion in keeping with the strong policy favoring settlement efforts. Service of process fees were reasonably incurred. The costs claimed on line 11 and the amounts included in line 13 relating to document discovery and Federal Express charges are not recoverable under the costs statute. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling including the dollar amounts of what is allowed and disallowed and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. = (302/HEK)

Defendants are awarded $10,000 in fees as to court's estimate of fees incurred by defendants due to the plaintiff's inclusion in his complaint and first amended complaint a prayer for a declaration that the easement is invalid. Such fees are recoverable under the easement agreement. All other fees sought by defendants are denied. This is not a proper motion to determine the award of non-fees costs. Such costs will be adjudicated on plaintiff's motion to tax costs. rulings.Case Title: STEVEN A. BURMEISTER, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE VS. 4055 SONOMA PROPERTIES, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Bart Jones' Joinder In The Motion To Fix Attorney'S Fees.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 5, 2013, Line 20, Defendant Bart Jones' Joinder In The Motion To Fix Attorney's Fees. Granted in part. Mr. Jones is awarded all the fees he seeks except for the fees incurred in communicating with a private investigator to locate Mr. Engdahl (4.75 hours) and the hour attributed to Blackstone discovery. Mr. Jones is entitled to fees under the sales agreement as the prevailing party on all the tort claims alleged against him. All fees sought including fees for litigating the fees and costs issues (except the 5.75 hours mentioned previously) were reasonably incurred. The hourly rate sought is reasonable. This is not a proper motion to determine the award of non-fees costs. Such costs will be adjudicated on plaintiff's motion to tax costs. Counsel for Mr. Jones is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing explicitly stating the amount of fees awarded even if the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ERON BLOCK VS. CLEAR CHANNEL BROADCASTING, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 5, 2013, Line 12, PLAINTIFF ERON BLOCK's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is denied. First, Plaintiff's separate statement does not comply with CCP 437c or CRC 3.1350. Plaintiff does not cite any evidence. Second, Plaintiff refers to Defendant's Clear Channel Outdoor Inc.'s ("CCO") response to the request for admissions, but does not provide the actual request. Third, Defendant CCO did not actually admit that there is an encroachment. Defendant denied the request and explained that there appeared to be an encroachment. Fourth, the motion violates CCP 437c(f)(1) because Plaintiff does not seek to dispose completely of the entirety of these two causes of action. Plaintiff presents no evidence regarding damages or entitlement to an injunction or any other relief. Fifth, Defendant correctly cites Kafka v. Bozio (1923) 191 Cal. 746, 750 and Pahl v. Ribero (1961) 193 Cal.App.2nd 154, 163l. An airspace encroachment is a nuisance, not a trespass. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ERON BLOCK VS. CLEAR CHANNEL BROADCASTING, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 5, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANTS CLEAR CHANNEL BROADCASTING, INC. and CLEAR CHANNEL MANAGEMENT SERVICES, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is granted as to Clear Channel Management Services, Inc. and Clear Channel Broadcasting, Inc. Moving parties have shown that they never constructed, owned, operated, maintained or hand any involvement in any billboard in the state of California, and that they never had any involvement with the billboard which allegedly encroaches onto property described as 1464 Kansas Street, San Francisco, CA, as alleged in the First Amended Complaint. The burden shifted to Plaintiff. No opposition was filed, and the motion for summary judgment is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ERON BLOCK VS. CLEAR CHANNEL BROADCASTING, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 5, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT CLEAR CHANNEL BROADCASTING, INC., CLEAR CHANNEL OUTDOOR, INC. and CLEAR CHANNEL MANAGEMENT SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Granted in part and denied in part. The Court grants the motion as to the claim for punitive damages. Defendants maintain their burden of production that they did not act with malice based upon the Reynolds and Qualls declarations. These declarations are sufficient to negate any charge of malice in the absence of counter declarations. See Tresemer v. Barke (1978) 86 Cal. App. 3d 656, 668. Plaintiff fails to present any countervailing evidence to create a triable issue of material fact on punitive damages. The Court denies the motion as to the issue of no compensatory damages. For this issue, Defendant relies solely on two matters deemed admitted by Plaintiff as to requests for admissions propounded by co-defendant Bell Plumbing and Heating North ("BPH"). (Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. B; Order Deeming Matters Admitted filed May 10, 2012) These matters deemed admitted are "You [meaning Plaintiff] have not been damaged by the alleged "dangerous conditions" posed by the billboard in your complaint," and "You [meaning Plaintiff] have not been damaged in any way due to the alleged encroachment in your complaint." (Id.) However, moving Defendants fail to demonstrate that the relationship between BPH and the remaining defendants supports use of these admissions by Defendants here. Defendants were non-requesting parties as to these admissions. Defendants have no relationship with BPH. BPH is not even named on the trespass cause of action (8th and 9th causes of action). Damages are not an element of trespass. The term "damaged" was not defined in the request for admissions. Swedberg v. Christiana Community Builders (1985) 175 Cal. App. 3d 138, relied on by Defendants, is distinguishable. There the Court allowed a parent company co-defendant to use requests for admission propounded by its affiliated company as against the plaintiff, and in so doing noted that the two co-defendants' liability were "tied directly" as a result of their relationship. Id. at 144. The Court in Swedberg noted that "[h]ypothetically, there may be a case where multiple defendants with diverse interests and separate theories of liability should be treated separately for all purposes." Even if these admissions were admissible as evidence in this motion for summary adjudication, Defendants have not shifted the burden because "damaged" is not defined, and the admission is too vague to be dispositive. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CAMILLOCCI VS. SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL, A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Deft Dignity Health To Provide Further Resposnes To Pltf'S First Request For Production Of Documents And Produce All Requested Non-Priveleged Documents, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 5, 2013, Line 13, PLAINTIFF JOSEPH CAMILLOCCI'S Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Dignity Health To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff's First Request For Production Of Documents And Produce All Requested Non-Priveleged Documents, And For Sanctions. Continued to March 14, 2013 for further briefing as set forth herein. The Court previously ordered the parties to file joint separate statements. However, the parties complied with the order by filing joint separate statements which are 929 pages long for the production requests propounded on Dignity Health and 267 pages long for the production requests propounded on Saint Francis Memorial Hospital. The purpose of a separate statement is for each party to state its arguments succinctly and convincingly. (See Weil & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial, 8:1151.1.) Here, the responses in the joint separate statements are repetitive, derivative and unwieldy, making it impractical for the court to rule on the matters in dispute. In order to avoid the cost of reference, the parties are ordered to file and serve a brief in support of and a brief in opposition to the requests for production still at each. Each brief shall be no longer than 15 pages. The discovery disputes shall be grouped by category, and the heading of each section shall state the general subject matter and, immediately thereafter, the numbers of each request for production falling within that category. (See Weil & Brown, supra, at 8:1154 - 8:1154.1.) Plaintiff shall file and serve by email his brief in support of the motion by no later than February 20, 2013 at 4:00 pm. Defendants shall file and serve by email any brief in opposition no later than March 4, 2013 at 4:00 pm. No reply shall be filed. Courtesy copies are to be provided to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, on the date of filing. The parties are encouraged to continue their meet and confer efforts to resolve their discovery disputes informally. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of Plaintiff's counsel in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DIANE CONNELL VS. HAR-BRO OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To File Anemded Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, February 5, 2013, Line 14, PLAINTIFF DIANE CONNELL's Motion For Leave To File Anemded Complaint. Granted without prejudice to any party moving to continue the trial date in Department 206. There is good cause to grant leave to file an amended complaint. Any substantive challenge to the amended complaint is more appropriately determined by way of a demurrer or motion for summary judgment or summary adjudication. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)
